tasks.title: Establish Project Infrastructure and Configuration System
tasks.description: Set up the modular Python project structure, environment management, and a centralized configuration system to drive the ML pipeline.
tasks.details: Initialize a Python environment using 'venv' or 'conda'. Create a directory structure: 'src/' for core logic, 'config/' for YAML/JSON settings, 'data/' for raw/processed datasets, and 'models/' for checkpoints. Implement a configuration manager using 'python-dotenv' and 'PyYAML' to load hyperparameters and system paths. Use 'pyproject.toml' for dependency management including 'torch' (or 'tensorflow'), 'pandas', 'scikit-learn', and 'fastapi'.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that all directories are created and that the configuration manager correctly loads values from a sample YAML file into a Python object. Ensure 'pip install .' works correctly.

tasks.title: Develop Data Ingestion and Preprocessing Pipeline
tasks.description: Create modules to load raw data, perform cleaning, handle missing values, and execute feature transformations.
tasks.details: Implement a 'DataProcessor' class in 'src/data_pipeline.py'. Use 'pandas' for CSV/JSON ingestion. Include methods for: 1. Cleaning (removing duplicates), 2. Imputation (filling NaNs with mean/median), 3. Transformation (StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler), and 4. Splitting (train_test_split). Ensure the pipeline is reproducible by saving transformation parameters.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test each cleaning and transformation method with edge cases (e.g., all null columns). Integration test the full pipeline from raw file to scaled training tensors.

tasks.title: Implement Modular Neural Network Architecture
tasks.description: Design a flexible neural network class that allows for configurable layers and activation functions based on input features.
tasks.details: Define a 'NeuralNet' class inheriting from 'torch.nn.Module'. Use the configuration file to determine the number of hidden layers, neurons per layer, and activation functions (e.g., ReLU, Sigmoid). Ensure the input dimension dynamically adapts to the preprocessed features from Task 32. Use 'nn.Sequential' for modular assembly of the layers.
tasks.testStrategy: Instantiate the model with various configurations from the YAML file and perform a dummy forward pass with a tensor of the expected input shape to ensure no dimension mismatches.

tasks.title: Configure Loss Functions and Optimization Strategies
tasks.description: Set up the training objectives and parameter update logic using standard optimizers and learning rate schedulers.
tasks.details: Create a factory module to instantiate loss functions (e.g., 'nn.MSELoss', 'nn.CrossEntropyLoss') and optimizers (e.g., 'Adam', 'SGD') based on the PRD configuration. Implement a learning rate scheduler (e.g., 'StepLR' or 'ReduceLROnPlateau') to manage the training regime as specified in the PRD.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that the optimizer correctly updates model parameters during a single mock training step. Ensure the loss function returns a scalar tensor with a gradient function attached.

tasks.title: Build the Training Engine and Batch Processing Loop
tasks.description: Implement the core training loop including epoch control, batching, forward/backward passes, and parameter updates.
tasks.details: Develop a 'Trainer' class in 'src/engine.py'. Use 'torch.utils.data.DataLoader' for batching. The loop must iterate through epochs, perform 'optimizer.zero_grad()', 'loss.backward()', and 'optimizer.step()'. Include logic to track the running loss and report progress to the console/logger. Ensure integration with the DataProcessor output.
tasks.testStrategy: Run a training cycle on a tiny 'overfit' dataset (5-10 rows) and verify that the loss decreases significantly over 50 epochs.

tasks.title: Implement Model Serialization and Checkpointing
tasks.description: Enable the system to save and restore model weights, optimizer states, and metadata to support resumed training and deployment.
tasks.details: Implement 'save_checkpoint' and 'load_checkpoint' functions. The checkpoint should be a dictionary containing: 'model_state_dict', 'optimizer_state_dict', 'epoch', and 'loss'. Save files as '.pth' or '.pt' in the 'models/' directory. Use timestamped filenames or 'best_model.pth' logic.
tasks.testStrategy: Train a model for 1 epoch, save it, modify the model weights, load the checkpoint, and assert that the loaded weights match the saved weights exactly.

tasks.title: Develop Evaluation Suite for Performance Metrics
tasks.description: Create a module to calculate Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1 Score, and Loss metrics on the validation/test set.
tasks.details: Implement an 'Evaluator' class. Use 'scikit-learn.metrics' for calculating multi-class or binary classification metrics (Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1). For regression, include RMSE/MAE. The module should produce a summary report and optionally save metrics to a JSON file for history tracking.
tasks.testStrategy: Compare the Evaluator's output against manually calculated metrics for a small fixed prediction set to ensure mathematical correctness.

tasks.title: Build Inference Engine and Prediction API
tasks.description: Expose the trained model via a REST API using FastAPI to serve real-time predictions.
tasks.details: Create 'app/main.py' using 'FastAPI'. Implement a POST endpoint '/predict' that: 1. Accepts a JSON payload of features, 2. Preprocesses the input using the saved transformation parameters from Task 32, 3. Loads the latest checkpoint, 4. Runs a forward pass in 'eval' mode, 5. Returns the prediction result as JSON.
tasks.testStrategy: Use 'pytest' and 'httpx' to send a POST request to the local API server and verify the response structure and status code (200 OK).

tasks.title: Integrate Logging and System Monitoring
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive logging for training progress, loss tracking, and system events.
tasks.details: Configure the standard 'logging' library to output to both 'logs/system.log' and stdout. Use 'matplotlib' or 'seaborn' to generate a 'loss_curve.png' and 'metrics_plot.png' at the end of training. Log key events: data loading start, training epoch end, evaluation results, and API request errors.
tasks.testStrategy: Check the 'logs/' directory after a training run to ensure the log file exists and contains the expected epoch entries. Verify the generation of the loss curve image.

tasks.title: Create Deployment Wrapper and Documentation
tasks.description: Prepare the system for production using containerization and startup scripts.
tasks.details: Write a 'Dockerfile' that installs system dependencies, copies the source code, and sets the 'uvicorn' command for the API. Create a 'docker-compose.yml' for orchestration. Provide a 'README.md' with setup instructions, API documentation, and examples of how to run the training vs. inference modes.
tasks.testStrategy: Build the Docker image and run the container locally. Verify that the '/predict' endpoint is accessible from the host machine.
